item.title: 响应式网页设计第 2 天 | Day 2 of Responsive Web Design
item.description:
杂志：

在本节中，我应该借助 HTML 和 CSS 创建一个 Cafe Menu。 我必须在菜单中添加两个部分，一个是咖啡和甜点。

我学会了如何在<script>标签的帮助下在 HTML 本身中添加 CSS 代码。 但它变得难以管理，而是使用带有 .css 扩展名的单独文件并将其与<link>标记链接。

代码片段：

 <link href="styles.css" rel="stylesheet"/>

这里的rel定义了链接资源和文档 HTML 之间的关系。

背景：
我们可以使用background-color等属性来改变颜色， background-image来改变背景图像等等。

文本：
利润：
展示：
为了在微观层面上正确地改变样式，我们可以使用<div>并创建新的分区只是为了在它们上面添加新的样式属性。 此外，我们可以将class属性设置为样式并访问特定内容。

填充：
一些构成属性是padding-left 、 padding-right 、 padding-top 、 padding-bottom 。

字体：
还有一些。

在创建 Cafe Menu 时，我没有遇到任何挑战或错误。 所以，今天的会议非常顺利。

我还在我的GitHub上发布了这些笔记。

生产率：

生产力得分：7
享受分数：7

与昨天相比，我今天的工作效率更高，但仍有很大的改进空间。 我喜欢应对挑战和调试，因为它们让我可以更深入地研究。 虽然我对最终网页感到满意，但我发现它太简单了。

外部链接：

我电脑上的墙纸： Marko
生产力得分： Ruby Granger

我上课时听了一些音乐。



原文:

Journal:

In this section, I'm supposed to create a Cafe Menu with the help of HTML and CSS. I have to add two sections in the menu one for Coffee and Desserts.

I learnt how to add CSS codes inside the HTML itself with the help of <script> tag. But it gets difficult to manage, rather having separate file with .css extension and linking that with <link> tag.

Code Snipet:

<link href="styles.css" rel="stylesheet"/>

Here rel defines the relationship between linked resource and the document HTML.

Background:
background property sets the background. We can constiuent properties like background-color to change the color, background-image to change the backgorund image and many more.

Text:
text-align to set up the alignment of content.

Margin:
margin-left, margin-right for setting the margin in left and right.

Display:
display property for setting the element as block, inline and some more.

In order to properly change the styling at a micro level, we can use <div> and create new division just to add new styling properties on them. Also, we can class attribute to style and access specific contents.

Padding:
padding property to set padding on 4 sides of an element. Some constituent properties are padding-left, padding-right, padding-top, padding-bottom.

Font:
font-family, font-size these are properties to change the styling of fonts in an element. There are some more.

While creating the Cafe Menu, I didn't face any challenges or errors. So, it was a very smooth session today.

I also have published the notes on my GitHub.

Productivity:

Productivity Score: 7
Enjoyment Score: 7

Compared to yesterday, I had a more productive day today, but there is still a lot of room for improvement. I enjoy tackling challenges and debugging because they allow me to delve deeper. While I was satisfied with the final webpage, I found it to be too easy.

Outside Links:

Wallpaper on my PC: Marko
Productivity Score: Ruby Granger

Some Music while I took the lesson.
item.author: sleepyweb

item.title: 每个人都应该知道的 5 大 GraphQL 缺点 | Top 5 GraphQL Disadvantages Everyone Should Know
item.description:
它拥有许多优势，例如提供更有效的数据检索方式以及使开发人员能够以声明方式指定他们需要的数据。 然而，与任何技术一样，GraphQL 也有开发人员应该注意的缺点。 在本文中，我们将研究 GraphQL 的前 5 大缺点，并讨论它们如何影响您的开发过程。

验证

在 GQL 中，我们有一个模式，我们在其中描述请求（查询、变更、类型）。 它与 REST 有什么不同？ 毕竟，事实上，API端点变化的应用是不存在的。 但并非一切都是这样。 在最简单的示例中，您有应用端点，其中一些端点可供未授权用户使用，其余部分可供授权用户使用，并且这两部分不相交。 好的，显然，所有 API 端点都可用于经过身份验证的客户端。 在 GQL 上模拟它是行不通的。 例如，您有一个方案，其中大多数请求对未经授权的用户不可用 - 直到他调用它们，才有办法知道它。 这就是为什么我们从方案中不清楚如何使用 API。

突变堆

如果查询的构建方式使得我们可以清楚地了解我们可以从哪个实体开始编写查询以及最终我们实际上可以获得什么，那么 Mutations 就有问题了。 我们打开 GQL 模式文档，看到大量可用于与服务通信的操作......

而且我需要多说一点，大多数情况下，开发人员为 Mutations 选择了非常糟糕的命名，并且很难在文档中找到连接到同一实体的所有操作，例如 GitHub GQL API。

文件

只要我们通过 GQL 交换数据，一切都很好。 但是迟早，您将需要下载文件。 是的，我们有一个multipart/form-data ，我们可以在其中将流文件附加到请求并让服务器支持它。 恭喜，这违反了 GQL 的基本方法之一 - 传输不可知论。 错误处理

该错误可能是以下两种类型之一：

  • 错误- 业务逻辑错误，和
  • 异常——这意味着你对一些错误的数据、实体等做了一些错误的操作。

在异常的情况下，我们有顶级异常的概念，当我们被直接告知：“请求因某某原因而失败，因为......”。

对于业务逻辑错误，情况有所不同：您必须自己描述不同情况下每个突变的错误。 但它们将采用何种格式，将携带何种信息——这些都没有标准化。

缓存

当我们使用 CRUD 时，我们有一个可用于请求的 HTTP 方法列表（GET、POST 等）。

拒绝数据幂等性剥夺了我们非常高的准确性——当我们以粗略的形式执行请求时，我们可以返回一个Cache头并指示我们应该缓存一些数据一段时间，因此浏览器将其放入缓存并选择为我们准备好数据。 显然，这加快了服务器在客户端-服务器通信中的响应速度。 但是对于 GQL，我们没有官方的解决方案。

结论

总之，虽然 GraphQL 与传统的 REST API 相比有很多好处，但它也有开发人员应该注意的缺点。 其中包括身份验证的挑战、变异堆管理的困难、文件处理的限制、各种错误处理方法以及缺乏标准化的缓存方法。 然而，通过仔细考虑和规划，开发人员可以克服这些挑战并利用 GraphQL 必须提供的许多优势。



原文:

GraphQL has quickly become a popular alternative to REST API for developers. It boasts a number of advantages, such as providing a more efficient way to retrieve data and enabling developers to declaratively specify what data they need. However, like any technology, GraphQL has its disadvantages that developers should be aware of. In this article, we will examine the top 5 GraphQL disadvantages and discuss how they can impact your development process.

Authentication

In GQL we have a schema in which we describe requests (queries, mutations, types). GQL is based on the assumption that this schema is static - not changing from the point of view of the application.

Is it different from REST? After all, in fact, there is no application in which API endpoints change. But not everything is like that. In the simplest example, you have app endpoints, some of which are available to an unauthorized user, and the rest, which are available to an authorized one, and these two parts do not intersect. Okay, obviously, all the API endpoints are available for an authenticated client. It will not work to simulate this on GQL. For example, you have a scheme in which most of the requests are not available to an unauthorized user - until he calls them, there is no way to know about it. That’s why it is not clear to us from the scheme how to use the API.

Mutations heap

GQL resembles an RPC (remote procedure call) - when we call some function and expect to get the result of execution

If Queries are built in such a way that it is clear to understand from what entity we could start writing a query and what actually we could get at the end, then there is a problem with Mutations. We open a GQL schema documentation and see a great heap of operations available to communicate with the service…

And I need to say more, mostly, the developers choose really bad naming for the Mutations and it’s really difficult to find all the operations connected to the same entity in the documentation, e.g. GitHub GQL API.

Files

As long as we exchange data via GQL, all is well. But sooner or later, you will need to download files. Yes, we have a multipart/form-data where we can attach a stream file to a request and let the server support it. My congratulations, this is a violation of one of the fundamental approaches of GQL - transport agnostic. GQL does not care what they work with: WebSockets, HTTP, carrier pigeons, etc.

GQL does not provide an answer to this question.

Error handling

The error could be one of two types:

  • Error - business logic error, and
  • Exception - which means you do some wrong operation with some wrong data, entity, etc.

In the case of exceptions, we have the concept of Top Level exception, when we are directly told: “The request failed for such and such a reason, because ...”.

With business logic Errors, the situation is different: you yourself must describe errors for each mutation for different cases. But in what format they will be, what information they will carry - it is not standardized.

Caching

When we used the CRUD, we had a list of available HTTP methods for our requests (GET, POST, etc.).

GQL denies all these patterns, it has not heard of idempotency, and it sends all requests using the POST HTTP method. The denial of data idempotency deprives us of very high accuracy - when we executed a request in a rough form, we could return a Cache header and indicate that we should cache some data for a while, so the browser put it in the cache and pick up ready-made data for us. Obviously, this speeded up responses from the server in client-server communication. But for GQL we don't have an official solution.

Conclusion

In conclusion, while GraphQL offers many benefits over traditional REST APIs, it also has its share of disadvantages that developers should be aware of. These include challenges with authentication, difficulty with mutation heap management, limitations with file handling, varied error handling approaches, and a lack of standardized caching methods. However, with careful consideration and planning, developers can overcome these challenges and leverage the many advantages that GraphQL has to offer.
